lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਰਾਇਣੁ ਸੁਪ੍ਰਸੰਨ ਹੋਇ ਤ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਨਾਮਾ ॥੩॥੧॥
lines.translit: naaraaein suprasan hoe ta sevak naamaa |3|1|
lines.english: If the Lord were totally pleased, then He would let Naam Dayv be His servant. ||3||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਭ ਲਹਰਿ ਅਤਿ ਨੀਝਰ ਬਾਜੈ ॥ ਕਾਇਆ ਡੂਬੈ ਕੇਸਵਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: lobh lehar at neejhar baajai | kaaeaa ddoobai kesavaa |1|
lines.english: The tidal waves of greed constantly assault me. My body is drowning, O Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਸਮੁੰਦੇ ਤਾਰਿ ਗੋੁਬਿੰਦੇ ॥ ਤਾਰਿ ਲੈ ਬਾਪ ਬੀਠੁਲਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sansaar samunde taar goubinde | taar lai baap beetthulaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Please carry me across the world-ocean, O Lord of the Universe. Carry me across, O Beloved Father. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਲ ਬੇੜਾ ਹਉ ਖੇਵਿ ਨ ਸਾਕਉ ॥ ਤੇਰਾ ਪਾਰੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ਬੀਠੁਲਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: anil berraa hau khev na saakau | teraa paar na paaeaa beetthulaa |2|
lines.english: I cannot steer my ship in this storm. I cannot find the other shore, O Beloved Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਹੁ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮੇਲਿ ਤੂ ਮੋ ਕਉ ॥ ਪਾਰਿ ਉਤਾਰੇ ਕੇਸਵਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: hohu deaal satigur mel too mo kau | paar utaare kesavaa |3|
lines.english: Please be merciful, and unite me with the True Guru; Carry me across, O Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮਾ ਕਹੈ ਹਉ ਤਰਿ ਭੀ ਨ ਜਾਨਉ ॥ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਬਾਹ ਦੇਹਿ ਬਾਹ ਦੇਹਿ ਬੀਠੁਲਾ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: naamaa kahai hau tar bhee na jaanau | mo kau baah dehi baah dehi beetthulaa |4|2|
lines.english: Says Naam Dayv, I do not know how to swim. Give me Your Arm, give me Your Arm, O Beloved Lord. ||4||2||
The body moves along like the ball of dung, driven on by the dung-beetle.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜ ਅਵਲਿ ਧੂੜਿ ਮਣੀ ਗਾਡੀ ਚਾਲਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: sehaj aval dhoorr manee gaaddee chaalatee |
lines.english: Slowly at first, the body-cart loaded with dust starts to move.
Later, it is driven on by the stick. ||1||
The beloved soul goes down to the pool to wash itself clean. ||1||Pause||
My mind is imbued with the Lord's Lotus Feet. ||2||
The washerman washes, imbued with the Lord's Love.
Please be kind to Your devotee. ||3||3||
Prays Naam Dayv, O Lord, You are All-pervading.
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Basant, The Word Of Ravi Daas Jee:
The proud bride shall not find a place with the Lord.
You know nothing.
Above your head, the crow of death is cawing. ||1||
Seeing your clothes, you are so proud of yourself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਭਾਦਉ ਖੂੰਬਰਾਜੁ ਤੂ ਤਿਸ ਤੇ ਖਰੀ ਉਤਾਵਲੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaise bhaadau khoonbaraaj too tis te kharee utaavalee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Even the mushrooms of summer live longer than you. ||1||Pause||
Why are you so proud? You are insane.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪ ਤਨ ਕਾ ਜੋ ਕਰੇ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: ap tan kaa jo kare beechaar |
lines.english: Whoever reflects on his own body
- the Messenger of Death does not abuse him. ||2||
The deer does not know the secret;
the musk is within its own body, but it searches for it outside.
his Lord and Master shall call for his account.
The man is so proud of his sons and his wife;
The soul suffers in pain for the actions it has committed.
If you seek the Support of the Holy,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੇ ਮਿਟਹਿ ਪਾਪ ਸਭ ਕੋਟਿ ਕੋਟਿ ॥
lines.translit: tere mitteh paap sabh kott kott |
lines.english: millions upon millions of your sins shall be totally erased.
Afterwards, whom shall you call, "Dear, Dear." ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਜਾਤਿ ਨ ਜਨਮੁ ਨ ਜੋਨਿ ਕਾਮੁ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis jaat na janam na jon kaam |4|1|
lines.english: is not concerned with social class, birth and rebirth. ||4||1||
Says Ravi Daas, one who chants the Naam, the Name of the Lord,
Basant, Kabeer Jee:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸ ਘਰ ਮਹਿ ਹੈ ਸੁ ਤੂ ਢੂੰਢਿ ਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: eis ghar meh hai su too dtoondt khaeh |
lines.english: Look around, and eat anything in this house.
The hair on your tail is shiny and lustrous. ||1||
You walk like a cow.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਉਰ ਕਿਸ ਹੀ ਕੇ ਤੂ ਮਤਿ ਹੀ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aaur kis hee ke too mat hee jaeh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: But do not go out to any other. ||1||Pause||
You lick the grinding bowl, and eat the flour.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਕੀ ਕਾ ਚੀਥਰਾ ਕਹਾਂ ਲੈ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: chaakee kaa cheetharaa kahaan lai jaeh |2|
lines.english: Where have you taken the kitchen rags? ||2||
Your gaze is fixed on the basket in the cupboard.
Says Kabeer, you have over-indulged in your pleasures.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਤੁ ਲਕਰੀ ਸੋਟਾ ਤੇਰੀ ਪਰੈ ਪੀਠਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: mat lakaree sottaa teree parai peetth |3|
lines.english: Watch out - a stick may strike you from behind. ||3||
Watch out - someone may throw a brick at you. ||4||1||
